notes.anchor: the proceedings I am sure are being sent to you
notes.note: Renders ex actis: the acta, the official Roman gazette recording senatorial business and public news, regularly dispatched to provincial commanders such as Cassius.

notes.anchor: the criminality of your kinsman Lepidus
notes.note: M. Aemilius Lepidus, governor of Narbonese Gaul and Nearer Spain, joined his army to Antony's in late May 43 BC and was branded a public enemy. He is Cassius's adfinis (kinsman by marriage) through his wife Junia, half-sister of M. Brutus.

notes.anchor: with the war finished --- as we supposed --- we are now waging a renewed war
notes.note: After Antony's defeat at Mutina (April 43 BC) the war seemed over, but Lepidus's defection in May joined his legions to Antony's and reopened the conflict.

notes.anchor: we have placed all our hope in D. Brutus and Plancus
notes.note: Decimus Brutus, relieved at Mutina, and L. Munatius Plancus, governor of Long-haired Gaul, were the republican commanders expected to crush Antony and Lepidus in Gaul.

notes.anchor: About Dolabella we were hearing here what we wanted to hear
notes.note: P. Cornelius Dolabella, the Caesarian who had murdered Trebonius, was the republican enemy in the East; Cicero hopes for word that Cassius has crushed him (he was besieged at Laodicea in Syria).
